summary: n8n-nodes-task-runner exfiltrates kubeconfig and opens reverse shell to 103.27.109.184 on require()
details: On require() of the package's main module, an IIFE in index.js reads the installer's Kubernetes credentials from /home/ubuntu/.kube/config or /root/.kube/config, copies them to /tmp/kubeconfig, and shells out to kubectl to enumerate pods, services, nodes, namespaces, and secrets across all namespaces. The collected kubeconfig, cluster secrets, and host reconnaissance (hostname and internal IPs via `hostname` / `hostname -I`) are sent over a raw TCP socket to the hardcoded address 103.27.109.184:8890. The same IIFE additionally spawns /bin/bash with a /dev/tcp reverse-shell one-liner connecting to 103.27.109.184:8895, granting interactive remote shell access on the installer host. The package name resembles n8n community node packages and its description is a generic 'Utils'; the hardcoded C2 IP is unrelated to any n8n publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
